advisories.title: Potassium acute electrolyte frame is carried ONLY by s24 — must not be retired or collapsed
advisories.detail: pkg_s24_creatinine_high_renal/signal_library.yaml:56-60 carries the creatinine+potassium arrhythmia escalation rule (or_renal_acute_imbalance, potassium > 5.2). This frame is completely absent from pkg_kb52c and the promoted candidate. The sprint's success criteria #4 ('s24 eGFR and potassium contexts are preserved, not deleted') and the prompt's known medical framing section (lines 165-168) make preservation mandatory. Any adjudication decision that classifies this frame as 'superseded' or 'covered' by Pass_3 packages would be factually incorrect. Cursor must classify this as an unadjudicated legacy frame requiring future Pass_3 enrichment.

advisories.title: eGFR CKD stage 3 escalation rule absent from Pass_3 — must be documented as coverage gap
advisories.detail: pkg_s24_creatinine_high_renal/signal_library.yaml:44-51 has an explicit escalation override (eGFR < 60 → at_risk). pkg_kb52c treats eGFR only as a supporting_metric corroborator without a threshold override. The adjudication decision YAML must document this as a known coverage gap. The two packages are not equivalent for severity escalation purposes.

advisories.title: Promoted candidate manifest description is factually incorrect — must be noted in decision document
advisories.detail: pkg_creatinine_high_renal_pass3_v1/package_manifest.yaml:4 says 'supersedes s24 authority by governance decision' — this is false. The authoritative fields (pilot_status: compiled_not_promoted_candidate, promotion_scope: non_runtime_candidate) override the free-text description. Cursor must record this contradiction in the decision document and confirm the compiled_not_promoted_candidate status. Cursor should NOT edit the manifest file itself (out-of-scope modification risk).

advisories.title: Promoted candidate uses schema_version 1.0.0 vs pkg_kb52c schema_version 2.0.0
advisories.detail: This schema version divergence is a structural artefact of the pilot compiler and does not affect clinical equivalence — both express the same signal logic. Cursor should note it in the comparison table but it is not a medical or governance concern.
